title: Accreditation and Registration of Foreign Placement Agencies, Staffing/Sourcing Companies, and Principal/Employers
content:
B.1. For Foreign Placement Agencies (FPAs): Requirements include business license or valid commercial registration of the actual employer hiring skilled Overseas Filipino Workers (OFWs); master employment contract signed by FPA and employer; manpower request; service agreement; escrow account with authorized bank for domestic worker hiring FPAs with deposit of USD50,000 to cover valid claims; and undertaking to monitor employment and report significant incidents.

B.2. For Staffing/Sourcing Companies: Requirements include list of clients' names and addresses; manpower request; and service agreement with clients.

SECTION 97: Issuance of Accreditation Certificate by Administration/POLO upon full compliance, containing employer/company name, address, contact, authorized representative, validity period, and issuance date.

SECTION 98: Registration of Principal/Employer by POEA upon submission of POLO accreditation certificate and supporting documents.

SECTION 99: Registration of POCB-registered projects upon submission of project authorization and master employment contract.

SECTION 100: Accreditation validity is four (4) years.

SECTION 101: POLO may suspend accreditation for grounds including refusal to assist/repatriate distressed OFWs, violation of contractual obligations, risk of exploitation or danger to OFWs, or hiring minors below prescribed age.

SECTION 102: Suspension procedures include written comment within 2 days, notice of suspension up to 60 days, appeal process to DOLE within 15 days, notification to Administration, and possible disciplinary action.

SECTION 103: Suspension effect includes suspension of documentary processing at POLO and Administration.

SECTION 104: Automatic revocation of accreditation for expired business license or cessation of business over 1 year, mutual agreement to terminate, disqualification penalty, or failure to comply with accreditation undertakings.

SECTION 105: Revocation/cancellation procedures include verification, comment period of 5 days, evaluation, and written notification.

SECTION 106: Validity of manpower request/job order is two (2) years, subject to revalidation.

SECTION 107: Renewal of accreditation requires reconfirmed recruitment agreement and revalidated/new job order.

SECTION 108: Dual/multiple accreditation limits: FPAs may be accredited to two licensed recruitment agencies; employers up to five, with conditions on compensation uniformity, job order size, and undertakings.

SECTION 109: Transfer of accreditation allowed with conditions on compensation package, assumption of obligations, and no pending disciplinary cases; accreditation within 6 months after cancellation deemed transfer.

SECTION 110: Outstanding money claims do not prevent POLO or Administration from acting on registration/accreditation if public interest requires.

SECTION 111: Accreditation in countries with special agreements follows Administration guidelines.

SECTION 112: POLO's authority to accredit may be suspended by Administrator for negligence, non-compliance with procedures, or accreditation of watchlisted principals; ILAB/DOLE notified.

SECTION 113: Suspension lifting possible upon ILAB/DOLE recommendation.

No mention of repeal found in the text.
